sections.label: Section 1.1
sections.original: μᾶτερ ἐμά , τὸ τεόν , χρύσασπι Θήβα ,
sections.translation: My mother , Thebe of the golden shield , I shall place your interests above my lack of leisure . May rocky Delos , in whose praises I have poured myself out , not be indignant at me .

sections.label: Section 1.5
sections.original: τί φίλτερον κεδνῶν τοκέων ἀγαθοῖς ;
sections.translation: What is dearer to good men than their noble parents ? Yield , island of Apollo ; indeed , with the help of the gods I shall accomplish the end of both graceful songs ,

sections.label: Section 1.7
sections.original: καὶ τὸν ἀκειρεκόμαν Φοῖβον χορεύων
sections.translation: honoring in the dance both Phoebus with the unshorn hair , in wave-washed Ceos with its mariners , and the sea-dividing reef of the Isthmus .

sections.label: Section 1.10
sections.original: δειράδʼ · ἐπεὶ στεφάνους
sections.translation: Since the Isthmus gave to the people of Cadmus six garlands from her games , the glory of triumph for my fatherland , where Alcmena bore her fearless

sections.label: Section 1.13
sections.original: παῖδα , θρασεῖαι τόν ποτε Γηρυόνα φρῖξαν κύνες .
sections.translation: son , before whom the bold hounds of Geryon once trembled . But I , while I frame for Herodotus a prize of honor for his four-horse chariot ,

sections.label: Section 1.15
sections.original: 20 ἁνία τʼ ἀλλοτρίαις οὐ χερσὶ νωμάσαντʼ ἐθέλω
sections.translation: and for managing the reins with his own hands and not another ’ s , want to join him to the song of Castor or of Iolaus , for of all heroes they were the strongest charioteers , the one born in Sparta and the other in Thebes .

sections.label: Section 1.18
sections.original: ἔν τʼ ἀέθλοισι θίγον πλείστων ἀγώνων ,
sections.translation: And in the games they attempted the greatest number of contests , and adorned their homes with tripods

sections.label: Section 1.20
sections.original: καὶ λεβήτεσσιν φιάλαισί τε χρυσοῦ ,
sections.translation: and caldrons and goblets of gold , tasting victorious garlands . Their excellence shines clearly , in the naked footraces and in the shield-clashing hoplite races ,

sections.label: Section 1.24
sections.original: οἷά τε χερσὶν ἀκοντίζοντες αἰχμαῖς ,
sections.translation: and in all the deeds of their hands , in flinging the spear

sections.label: Section 1.25
sections.original: καὶ λιθίνοις ὁπότʼ ἐν δίσκοις ἵεν .
sections.translation: and whenever they hurled the stone discus . For there was no pentathlon , but for each feat a separate prize was set up . Often crowning their hair with wreaths from these contests they appeared beside the streams of Dirce or near the Eurotas ,

sections.label: Section 1.30
sections.original: 40 Ἰφικλέος μὲν παῖς ὁμόδαμος ἐὼν Σπαρτῶν γένει ,
sections.translation: the son of Iphicles , who was of the same city as the race of the Sown Men , and the son of Tyndareus , dwelling among the Achaeans in his highland home of Therapne . Farewell . But I , arraying with song Poseidon and the sacred Isthmus and the shores of Onchestus , shall tell , along with the honors of this man , the very famous fortune of his father Asopodorus

sections.label: Section 2.1
sections.original: οἱ μὲν πάλαι , ὦ Θρασύβουλε , φῶτες , οἳ χρυσαμπύκων
sections.translation: The men of old , Thrasybulus , who mounted the chariot of the Muses with their golden headbands , joining the glorious lyre , lightly shot forth their honey-voiced songs for young men , if one was handsome and had

sections.label: Section 2.5
sections.original: εὐθρόνου μνάστειραν ἁδίσταν ὀπώραν .
sections.translation: the sweetest ripeness that brings to mind Aphrodite on her lovely throne .

sections.label: Section 2.6
sections.original: 10 ἁ Μοῖσα γὰρ οὐ φιλοκερδής πω τότʼ ἦν οὐδʼ ἐργάτις ·
sections.translation: For in those days the Muse was not yet a lover of gain , nor did she work for hire . And sweet gentle-voiced odes did not go for sale , with silvered faces , from honey-voiced Terpsichore . But as things are now , she bids us heed

sections.label: Section 2.10
sections.original: ῥῆμʼ ἀλαθείας ἐτᾶς ἄγχιστα βαῖνον ,
sections.translation: the saying of the Argive man , which comes closest to actual truth :

sections.label: Section 2.11
sections.original: χρήματα , χρήματʼ ἀνήρ , ὃς φᾶ κτεάνων θʼ ἅμα λειφθεὶς καὶ φίλων .
sections.translation: “ Money , money makes the man ,” he said , when he lost his wealth and his friends at the same time . But enough , for you are wise . I sing the Isthmian victory with horses , not unrecognized , which Poseidon granted to Xenocrates ,

sections.label: Section 2.15
sections.original: Δωρίων αὐτῷ στεφάνωμα κόμᾳ
sections.translation: and sent him a garland of Dorian wild celery for his hair , to have himself crowned ,

sections.label: Section 2.17
sections.original: εὐάρματον ἄνδρα γεραίρων , Ἀκραγαντίνων φάος .
sections.translation: thus honoring the man of the fine chariot , the light of the people of Acragas . And in Crisa widely powerful Apollo looked graciously on him , and gave him glory there as well . And joined with the renowned favors of the sons of Erechtheus

sections.label: Section 2.20
sections.original: 30 ταῖς λιπαραῖς ἐν Ἀθάναις , οὐκ ἐμέμφθη
sections.translation: in splendid Athens , he found no fault with the chariot-preserving hand of the man who drove his horses ,

sections.label: Section 2.22
sections.original: τὰν Νικόμαχος κατὰ καιρὸν νεῖμʼ ἁπάσαις ἁνίαις ·
sections.translation: the hand with which Nicomachus gave the horses full rein at the right moment — that driver whom the heralds of the seasons , the Elean truce-bearers of Zeus son of Cronus recognized , since they had no doubt experienced some hospitable act of friendship from him .

sections.label: Section 2.25
sections.original: ἁδυπνόῳ τέ νιν ἀσπάζοντο φωνᾷ
sections.translation: And with sweet-breathing voice they greeted him when he fell into the lap of golden Victory

sections.label: Section 2.27
sections.original: 40 γαῖαν ἀνὰ σφετέραν , τὰν δὴ καλέοισιν Ὀλυμπίου Διὸς
sections.translation: in their own land , which they call the precinct of Olympian Zeus , where the sons of Aenesidamus were linked with immortal honors .

sections.label: Section 2.30
sections.original: καὶ γὰρ οὐκ ἀγνῶτες ὑμῖν ἐντὶ δόμοι
sections.translation: Truly , Thrasybulus , the homes of your family are not unfamiliar with lovely victory-processions , nor with the sweet boasting of songs .

sections.label: Section 3.1
sections.original: εἴ τις ἀνδρῶν εὐτυχήσαις ἢ σὺν εὐδόξοις ἀέθλοις
sections.translation: If any man has good fortune , either in famous contests or by the strength of his wealth , yet restrains troublesome ambition in his mind , he is worthy to be joined with his townsmen ’ s praises . Zeus , great excellence attends on mortals

sections.label: Section 3.5
sections.original: ἐκ σέθεν · ζώει δὲ μάσσων ὄλβος ὀπιζομένων , πλαγίαις δὲ φρένεσσιν
sections.translation: from you . Greater prosperity lives with those who revere you ; but it does not keep company with crooked minds , flourishing equally for all time .

sections.label: Section 3.7
sections.original: εὐκλέων δʼ ἔργων ἄποινα χρὴ μὲν ὑμνῆσαι τὸν ἐσλόν ,
sections.translation: As a recompense for glorious deeds , it is right to celebrate a noble man , and it is right to exalt him in victory-songs with the gentle Graces . Yes , in two contests Melissus

sections.label: Section 3.10
sections.original: μοῖρα πρὸς εὐφροσύναν τρέψαι γλυκεῖαν
sections.translation: has had a share of good fortune , to turn his heart to sweet joyfulness ; he received garlands in the glens of the Isthmus , and in the valley of the deep-chested lion he had Thebes announced

sections.label: Section 3.13
sections.original: ἱπποδρομίᾳ κρατέων . ἀνδρῶν δʼ ἀρετὰν
sections.translation: when he was victorious in horse-racing . He does not dishonor the inborn excellence he has from his ancestors .

sections.label: Section 3.15
sections.original: ἴστε μὰν Κλεωνύμου
sections.translation: Surely you know of the ancient glory of Cleonymus in the chariot-races . And , being related to the Labdacids on their mother ’ s side , they followed a path of wealth with the toil of their four-horse teams . But the whirling days of a man ’ s lifetime change many things . Only the children of the gods are unwounded .

sections.label: Section 4.1
sections.original: ἔστι μοι θεῶν ἕκατι μυρία παντᾷ κέλευθος ·
sections.translation: Thanks to the gods , I have countless paths opening on every side ; Melissus , at the Isthmian games you revealed abundant resources for celebrating in song the excellence of your family , in which the sons of Cleonymus flourish perpetually ,

sections.label: Section 4.5
sections.original: σὺν θεῷ θνατὸν διέρχονται βιότου τέλος . ἄλλοτε δʼ ἀλλοῖος οὖρος
sections.translation: with a god ’ s favor , as they progress towards the mortal end of life . But a changeable wind sweeps down and drives all men at different times .

sections.label: Section 4.7
sections.original: τοὶ μὲν ὦν Θήβαισι τιμάεντες ἀρχᾶθεν λέγονται
sections.translation: These men truly are spoken of as honored in Thebes from the beginning ; they have good relations with the neighboring towns , and are bereft of loud arrogance . And as for the memorials that fly through all the world ,

sections.label: Section 4.10
sections.original: μαρτύρια φθιμένων ζωῶν τε φωτῶν
sections.translation: memorials of boundless fame for living and dead men , they have attained all of these in full . Through their manly deeds they reached from home to touch the farthest limit , the pillars of Heracles —

sections.label: Section 4.13
sections.original: καὶ μηκέτι μακροτέραν σπεύδειν ἀρετάν .
sections.translation: do not pursue excellence any farther than that ! And they became breeders of horses

sections.label: Section 4.15
sections.original: χαλκέῳ τʼ Ἄρει ἅδον .
sections.translation: and were pleasing to bronze-clad Ares . But on a single day the rough storm of war robbed their blessed hearth of four men . Now , after the wintry gloom of the changing months , the ground has blossomed as if with crimson roses

sections.label: Section 4.19
sections.original: δαιμόνων βουλαῖς . ὁ κινητὴρ δὲ γᾶς Ὀγχηστὸν οἰκέων
sections.translation: by the will of the gods . The shaker of the earth who dwells at Onchestus

sections.label: Section 4.20
sections.original: καὶ γέφυραν ποντιάδα πρὸ Κορίνθου τειχέων ,
sections.translation: and at the sea-bridge before the walls of Corinth , by offering to that family this marvellous song , wakes from her bed their ancient fame for glorious deeds . For she had fallen asleep , but now she has awakened and her body shines , marvellous to see , like the morning-star among other stars .

sections.label: Section 4.25
sections.original: ἅ τε κἀν γουνοῖς Ἀθανᾶν ἅρμα καρύξαισα νικᾶν
sections.translation: She proclaimed their chariot victorious on the high ground of Athens and also in Sicyon at the games of Adrastus , and thus gave them leaves of song , like these , from the singers of their time . Nor did they keep their curved chariot from competing in the general contests ; striving against all of Greece , they rejoiced in spending their wealth on their horses .

sections.label: Section 5.1
sections.original: μᾶτερ Ἁλίου πολυώνυμε Θεία ,
sections.translation: Mother of the Sun , Theia of many names , for your sake men honor gold as more powerful than anything else ;

sections.label: Section 5.5
sections.original: νᾶες ἐν πόντῳ καὶ ὑφʼ ἅρμασιν ἵπποι
sections.translation: and through the value you bestow on them , o queen , ships contending on the sea and yoked teams of horses in swift-whirling contests become marvels .

sections.label: Section 5.7
sections.original: ἔν τʼ ἀγωνίοις ἀέθλοισι ποθεινὸν
sections.translation: And in athletic contests , someone who has wreathed his hair with many garlands has achieved longed-for fame , when he has been victorious with his hands

sections.label: Section 5.10
sections.original: ἢ ταχυτᾶτι ποδῶν .
sections.translation: or with the swiftness of his feet . But the valor of men is judged by gods , and there are only two things that cultivate the sweetest flower of life in blossoming prosperity :

sections.label: Section 5.13
sections.original: εἴ τις εὖ πάσχων λόγον ἐσλὸν ἀκούῃ .
sections.translation: to have good fortune and a noble reputation . Do not seek to become Zeus ; you have everything ,

sections.label: Section 5.15
sections.original: εἴ σε τούτων μοῖρʼ ἐφίκοιτο καλῶν .
sections.translation: if a share of these fine things comes to you . Mortal aims befit mortal men . But for you , Phylacidas , flourishing twofold excellence is recorded at the Isthmus , and at Nemea for both you and Pytheas in the pancratium . But my heart

sections.label: Section 5.20
sections.original: οὐκ ἄτερ Αἰακιδᾶν κέαρ ὕμνων γεύεται ·
sections.translation: cannot taste songs without telling of the race of Aeacus . I have come with the Graces for the sons of Lampon

sections.label: Section 5.22
sections.original: τάνδʼ ἐς εὔνομον πόλιν . εἰ δὲ τέτραπται
sections.translation: to this well-governed city . If Aegina turns her steps to the clear road of god-given deeds , then do not grudge

sections.label: Section 5.25
sections.original: κιρνάμεν ἀντὶ πόνων .
sections.translation: to mix for her in song a boast that is fitting recompense for toils . In heroic times , too , fine warriors gained fame , and they are celebrated with lyres and flutes in full-voiced harmonies

sections.label: Section 5.28
sections.original: μυρίον χρόνον · μελέταν δὲ σοφισταῖς
sections.translation: for time beyond reckoning . Heroes who are honored by the grace of Zeus provide a theme for skilled poets :

sections.label: Section 5.30
sections.original: ἐν μὲν Αἰτωλῶν θυσίαισι φαενναῖς
sections.translation: among the Aetolians the brave sons of Oeneus are worshipped with shining sacrifices ; in Thebes the horseman Iolaus has his honor , and Perseus in Argos , and the spearman Castor together with Polydeuces by the streams of Eurotas .

sections.label: Section 6.1
sections.original: θάλλοντος ἀνδρῶν ὡς ὅτε συμποσίου
sections.translation: Just as we mix the second bowl of wine when the men ’ s symposium is flourishing , here is the second song of the Muses for Lampon ’ s children and their athletic victories : first in Nemea , Zeus , in your honor they received the choicest of garlands ,

sections.label: Section 6.5
sections.original: νῦν αὖτε Ἰσθμοῦ δεσπότᾳ
sections.translation: and now in honor of the lord of the Isthmus and the fifty Nereids , for the victory of the youngest son , Phylacidas . May there be a third libation of honey-voiced songs to pour over Aegina in honor of Zeus Soter of Olympia .

sections.label: Section 6.10
sections.original: εἰ γάρ τις ἀνθρώπων δαπάνᾳ τε χαρεὶς
sections.translation: For if a man , rejoicing in expense and toil , achieves godly excellence , and a divinity sows the seed of lovely fame in him , then he already casts his anchor on the farthest shore of prosperity , since he is honored by the gods . The son of Cleonicus prays that with such feelings

sections.label: Section 6.15
sections.original: ἀντιάσαις Ἀΐδαν γῆράς τε δέξασθαι πολιὸν
sections.translation: he will meet death and welcome gray old age . And I entreat Clotho , throned on high , and her sister Fates , to hear my friend ’ s prayers for fame .

sections.label: Section 6.19
sections.original: ὔμμε τʼ , ὦ χρυσάρματοι Αἰακίδαι ,
sections.translation: And as for you , sons of Aeacus with your golden chariots ,

sections.label: Section 6.20
sections.original: τέθμιόν μοι φαμὶ σαφέστατον ἔμμεν
sections.translation: I say that it is my clearest law to sprinkle you with praises whenever I set foot on this island . Countless continuous roads have been cut a hundred feet wide for your fine deeds , both beyond the springs of the Nile and through the land of the Hyperboreans . There is no city so barbarous or so strange in its speech

sections.label: Section 6.25
sections.original: ἅτις οὐ Πηλέος ἀΐει κλέος ἥρωος , εὐδαίμονος γαμβροῦ θεῶν ,
sections.translation: that it does not know the fame of the hero Peleus , the fortunate in-law of gods ,

sections.label: Section 6.26
sections.original: οὐδʼ ἅτις Αἴαντος Τελαμωνιάδα
sections.translation: or of Aias and his father Telamon . The son of Alcmena led him in ships to Troy , the toil of heroes , for war that delights in bronze , as an eager ally along with the men of Tiryns because of Laomedon ’ s wrongdoing .

sections.label: Section 6.30
sections.original: ἐν ναυσὶν Ἀλκμήνας τέκος .
sections.translation: He took Pergamos , and with Telamon ’ s help he slew the tribes of Meropes , and the herdsman Alcyoneus , huge as a mountain , whom he found at Phlegrae , and he did not keep his hands off the deep-voiced bow-string , not

sections.label: Section 7.1
sections.original: τίνι τῶν πάρος , ὦ μάκαιρα Θήβα ,
sections.translation: In which of the local glories of the past , divinely blessed Thebe , did you most delight your spirit ? Was it when you raised to eminence the one seated beside Demeter of the clashing bronze cymbals , flowing-haired

sections.label: Section 7.5
sections.original: ἄντειλας Διόνυσον ; ἢ χρυσῷ μεσονύκτιον νίφοντα δεξαμένα τὸν φέρτατον θεῶν ,
sections.translation: Dionysus ? Or when you received , as a snow-shower of gold in the middle of the night , the greatest of the gods ,

sections.label: Section 7.6
sections.original: ὁπότʼ Ἀμφιτρύωνος ἐν θυρέτροις
sections.translation: when he stood in the doorway of Amphitryon , and then went in to the wife to beget Heracles ? Or did you delight most in the shrewd counsels of Teiresias ? Or in the wise horseman Iolaus ?

sections.label: Section 7.10
sections.original: ἢ Σπαρτῶν ἀκαμαντολογχᾶν ; ἢ ὅτε καρτερᾶς Ἄδραστον ἐξ ἀλαλᾶς ἄμπεμψας ὀρφανὸν
sections.translation: Or in the Sown Men , untiring with the spear ? Or when you sent Adrastus back from the mighty war-shout , bereft

sections.label: Section 7.11
sections.original: μυρίων ἑτάρων ἐς Ἄργος ἵππιον ;
sections.translation: of countless companions , to Argos , home of horses ? Or because you stood upright on its feet the Dorian colony of the men of Lacedaemon , and your descendants ,

sections.label: Section 7.15
sections.original: Αἰγεῖδαι σέθεν ἔκγονοι , μαντεύμασι Πυθίοις ;
sections.translation: the Aegeids , captured Amyclae according to the Pythian oracles ? But since ancient grace sleeps , and mortals are forgetful

sections.label: Section 7.18
sections.original: ὅ τι μὴ σοφίας ἄωτον ἄκρον
sections.translation: of whatever does not reach the highest bloom of skillful song , joined to glorious streams of words ,

sections.label: Section 7.20
sections.original: κώμαζʼ ἔπειτεν ἁδυμελεῖ σὺν ὕμνῳ
sections.translation: then begin the victory procession with a sweet-singing hymn for Strepsiades ; for he is the victor in the pancratium at the Isthmus , both awesome in his strength and handsome to look at ; and he treats excellence as no worse a possession than beauty .

sections.label: Section 7.23
sections.original: φλέγεται δὲ ἰοπλόκοισι Μοίσαις ,
sections.translation: He is made radiant by the violet-haired Muses , and he has given a share in his flowering garland to his uncle and namesake ,

sections.label: Section 7.25
sections.original: χάλκασπις ᾧ πότμον μὲν Ἄρης ἔμιξεν ,
sections.translation: for whom Ares of the bronze shield mixed the cup of destiny ; but honor is laid up as recompense for good men . For let him know clearly , whoever , in this cloud of war , wards off the hailstorm of blood in defense of his dear fatherland

sections.label: Section 7.28
sections.original: λοιγὸν ἄντα φέρων ἐναντίῳ στρατῷ ,
sections.translation: by bringing destruction to the enemy host , that he is causing the greatest glory to grow for the race of his fellow-citizens ,

sections.label: Section 7.30
sections.original: ζώων τʼ ἀπὸ καὶ θανών .
sections.translation: in both his life and his death . And you , son of Diodotus , emulating the warrior Meleager , emulating Hector and Amphiaraus , breathed out your blossoming youth

sections.label: Section 8.1
sections.original: Κλεάνδρῳ τις ἁλικίᾳ τε λύτρον
sections.translation: Young men ! One of you go , in honor of Cleandros and his youth , to the splendid doorway of his father Telesarchus , and awake the victory-song , glorious recompense for his troubles , as a reward for his victory at the Isthmus , and

sections.label: Section 8.5
sections.original: 10 ἀέθλων ὅτι κράτος ἐξεῦρε . τῷ καὶ ἐγώ , καίπερ ἀχνύμενος θυμόν , αἰτέομαι χρυσέαν καλέσαι Μοῖσαν . ἐκ μεγάλων δὲ πενθ έω ν λυθέντες
sections.translation: because he found strength in the Nemean games . Therefore I too , though grieving in my heart , am asked to invoke the golden Muse . Released from great sorrows , let us not fall into bereavement of garlands ; do not nurse your pain . Having ceased from insurmountable troubles , we will sing something sweet for the people , even after toil . Since

sections.label: Section 8.10
sections.original: τὸν Ταντάλου λίθον παρά τις ἔτρεψεν ἄμμι θεός ,
sections.translation: one of the gods has turned aside for us the stone of Tantalus above our heads ,

sections.label: Section 8.11
sections.original: ἀτόλματον Ἑλλάδι μόχθον . ἀλλά
sections.translation: an unbearable hardship for Greece . But as for me , the passing away of terror has stopped my mighty worry . It is always better to look at what lies before one ’ s foot , in every case . For a treacherous lifetime hangs over men ’ s heads ,

sections.label: Section 8.15
sections.original: 30 ἑλίσσων βίου πόρον · ἰατὰ δʼ ἔστι βροτοῖς σύν γʼ ἐλευθερίᾳ καὶ τά . χρὴ δʼ ἀγαθὰν ἐλπίδʼ ἀνδρὶ μέλειν · χρὴ δʼ ἐν ἑπταπύλοισι Θήβαις τραφέντα
sections.translation: twisting around the path of life . Yet even this may be healed for mortals , if only they have freedom . It is right for a man to take to heart good hope ; and it is right for a man raised in seven-gated Thebes to offer the choicest bloom of the Graces to Aegina . For as twin daughters they were born to the same father , the youngest of Asopus ’ children , and they were pleasing to Zeus the king . He caused one of them to dwell beside the beautiful stream

sections.label: Section 8.20
sections.original: Δίρκᾳ φιλαρμάτου πόλιος ᾤκισσεν ἁγεμόνα ·
sections.translation: of Dirce , to lead a chariot-loving city ;

sections.label: Section 8.21
sections.original: σὲ δʼ ἐς νᾶσον Οἰνοπίαν ἐνεγκὼν
sections.translation: but he carried you to the island Oenopia and slept with you there , where you bore Aeacus , the dearest of all men on earth to the loud-thundering father . Aeacus settled disputes even for the gods . His god-like

sections.label: Section 8.25
sections.original: ἀρίστευον υἱέες υἱέων τʼ ἀρηΐφιλοι παῖδες ἀνορέᾳ χάλκεον στονόεντʼ ἀμφέπειν ὅμαδον · σώφρονές τʼ ἐγένοντο πινυτοί τε θυμόν .
sections.translation: sons and their sons , devoted to war , were the best in manliness , engaged in the brazen battle-throng that causes groans , and they were wise and prudent in spirit . All this was remembered even by the assembly of the blessed gods , when Zeus and splendid Poseidon contended for marriage with Thetis , each of them wanting her to be his lovely bride ; for desire possessed them .

sections.label: Section 8.30
sections.original: ἀλλʼ οὔ σφιν ἄμβροτοι τέλεσαν εὐνὰν θεῶν πραπίδες ,
sections.translation: But the immortal minds of the gods did not accomplish that marriage for them ,
